---
title: "Outside Resources"
subtitle: "Talks, guides, and links beyond the bootcamp itself"
---

This page is a running list of useful outside resources: talks, articles, demos, explainers, and people worth following. It is broader than the bootcamp itself.

## How to use this page

For each link, try to add:

- what it is
- why it is useful
- who it is for
- whether it is more about teaching, research, or general AI literacy

## Talks and explainers

### Paul Goldsmith-Pinkham

- [Getting started with Claude Code](https://paulgp.substack.com/p/getting-started-with-claude-code?utm_source=post-email-title&publication_id=1015181&post_id=192433164&utm_campaign=email-post-title&isFreemail=true&r=4oe7l&triedRedirect=true&utm_medium=email)
  A good starting point for faculty who want to understand what Claude Code is for, how it fits into a workflow, and why working in the terminal feels different from ordinary chat.

### Claude Blattman

- [claudeblattman.com](https://claudeblattman.com/)
  Useful for skills, templates, and Claude Code-oriented workflow examples. Good for people who want concrete patterns rather than abstract prompting advice.

## Tool documentation and guides

### Anthropic / Claude

- Claude app
- Claude Code
- prompt-writing or workflow guides

### OpenAI / ChatGPT / Codex

- ChatGPT guides
- Codex guides
- workflow examples

### Cursor

- [Cursor](https://cursor.com/)
  A popular AI code editor for people who want an editor-based workflow rather than a pure terminal workflow.
- [Cursor docs](https://docs.cursor.com/)
  Useful if you want to compare Cursor's editor-based workflow to Claude Code or Codex.

### Obsidian

- [Obsidian](https://obsidian.md/)
  A flexible notes app that works well as a personal knowledge base, project notebook, or place to store prompts, reusable instructions, and session notes.
- [Obsidian Help](https://help.obsidian.md/)
  Useful if you want to learn linking, vault structure, templates, and other features for organizing AI-assisted work over time.

### Microsoft / Copilot

- UVM Copilot access info
- Microsoft 365 Copilot guides for Word, Excel, PowerPoint, or Outlook

## Teaching-focused resources

- assignment design
- rubric design
- feedback workflows
- AI policy language

## Research-focused resources

- literature review workflows
- coding assistance
- data cleaning and exploration
- writing and revision
- paper-reading AI tool or paper-summarization service
  Placeholder: Emily has a specific reference in mind and will fill this in later.

## Notes

- Keep this page broader than the bootcamp itself.
- If a resource becomes central to a session, link it from the relevant session page too.
